0.title: Satellite Data Cubes
0.findings: The Open Data Cube (ODC) is the open-source standard for managing and analysing large EO archives; data cubes deliver Analysis-Ready Data (ARD) - standardised, harmonised, cloud-stored time series after geometric/radiometric/atmospheric correction, removing demanding pre-processing for the user. The Agriculture monitoring Data Cube (ADC) is an automated modular pipeline indexing optical and SAR imagery. The SITS 'time-first, space-later' paradigm organises big EO images as data cubes for satellite image time-series (SITS) land/crop classification.

refs.t: Open Data Cube (open-source EO data management)
A Data Cube of Big Satellite Image Time-Series for Agriculture Monitoring (2022)
SITS: Satellite Image Time Series Analysis on EO Data Cubes (e-sensing book)
Copernicus OBSERVER: Data cubes enabling EO applications

1.title: Field Ground Truth
1.findings: Supervised crop classification requires extensive ground-truth reference data for training and validation; the quality, quantity and spatial representativeness of reference labels fundamentally determine accuracy - sparse or spatially biased data impairs performance. Conventional ground-truthing (field surveys, census, photo-interpretation) is costly; modern strategies use GPS plot capture, geotagged photos and farmer interviews, with stratified sampling and route-optimised campaigns to cover spectrally complex zones. Scarcity of timely interoperable ground data is acute in smallholder systems.

refs.t: Strategic Ground Data Planning for Efficient Crop Classification (Geographies 2024)
Collecting and Disseminating Ground Truth Data for Generation of Agriculture Statistics
Scaling Ground Truth Data Collection for Advanced Crop Analytics (Tetra Tech)

2.title: Image Preprocessing Basics
2.findings: Atmospheric correction is essential pre-processing for biophysical retrieval, multi-temporal analysis and classification. Sentinel-2 Level-1C is Top-of-Atmosphere reflectance (radiometric + geometric/orthorectification corrections); Level-2A is Bottom-of-Atmosphere (atmospherically corrected) produced by SEN2COR, which also performs terrain and cirrus correction and scene classification (AOT and water-vapour retrieval, TOA->BOA). Radiometric calibration normalises sensor noise/striping. The Harmonized Landsat Sentinel-2 (HLS) dataset provides cross-sensor ARD surface reflectance.

refs.t: Impact of Atmospheric Corrections on Sentinel-2 Land-Cover Classification (MDPI IJGI 2020)
Harmonized Landsat and Sentinel-2 v2.0 surface reflectance dataset (RSE 2025)
Assessment of atmospheric correction methods for Sentinel-2 (ISPRS/IJAEOG)

3.title: Large-Scale Crop Monitoring
3.findings: Landsat (since 1972) is the longest operational agricultural-monitoring record. Operational national programmes include Canada's Annual Crop Inventory. The JRC ASAP system provides global anomaly hot-spot early warning for food security. FAO's STI portal catalogues satellite monitoring platforms. Operational pipelines fuse multispectral imagery, weather and machine learning for real-time crop health, production forecasting and market intelligence, supporting national statistics and SDG reporting.

refs.t: UN Handbook on Remote Sensing for Agricultural Statistics (FAO, official PDF)
ASAP: a new global early warning system for agricultural production (JRC)
FAO STI: Satellite- and Remote-Sensing-Based Agricultural Monitoring Platforms
NASA Earthdata: Satellite Remote Sensing for Agricultural Applications

4.title: Vegetation Indices Deep Dive
4.findings: NDVI is a simple, fast, non-destructive spectral index from red/near-infrared reflectance, the workhorse of crop status monitoring. Phenology metrics (start/peak/end of season) are derived per-pixel from time series such as MODIS 16-day NDVI and correlate with yield. NDVI is computed across MODIS, AVHRR, Landsat, Sentinel and UAV. Complementary indices include GNDVI (chlorophyll sensitivity), ARVI (atmospheric resistance) and SAVI (soil-brightness adjustment). Applications span yield prediction, irrigation scheduling, classification, change detection and phenology.

refs.t: Improving remotely-sensed crop monitoring by NDVI-based crop phenology estimators (Agric. & Forest Meteorology)
Prediction of Crop Yield Using Phenological Information from RS Vegetation Index
NDVI and Beyond: Vegetation Indices for Crop Recognition (Sensors 2025)
